name: Phase 1: Refactoring guide
description: Manual hook — safe refactoring patterns for Phase 1 code.

action.message:
You've triggered the Phase 1 Refactoring Guide.

## Safe Refactors
These changes are safe and won't break anything:
- Renaming internal helper functions (_get_client, _ensure_motors_ready, _move_for_duration) — update all call sites
- Extracting PLANNER_PROMPT sections into named sub-strings then joining — keep the final string identical
- Adding type annotations to existing functions
- Adding logging (use Python logging module, never print passwords)
- Extracting the fence-stripping logic in plan_navigation() into a helper function

## Risky Refactors (require full test after)
- Changing the @tool function signatures — Strands uses type hints for schema generation
- Changing PLANNER_PROMPT content — always test with 'Move forward 2 seconds' and 'Do a full circle' after
- Changing the execute_step() dispatch table — test all 6 actions
- Changing the Flask route paths — update frontend fetch URLs too
- Changing the execute results summary shape — update frontend result rendering

## Never Refactor
- Remove the finally: stop_car() block in _move_for_duration()
- Remove the confirmation gate in main.py or index.html
- Change tools=[] to include tool functions in create_planner()
- Change _get_client() to return an error string instead of raising RuntimeError for missing password
- Move .env loading out of module level (it must run before constants are set)

What refactoring are you planning?
